items.question: What does the One-Click Storyboard AI workflow do?
items.answer: This workflow turns scripts, ideas, or outlines into visual storyboards in a single run. It generates panel-by-panel sequences that help you see framing, action, and pacing before production.

items.question: What should I include in my script or outline for best results?
items.answer: Include key actions, dialogue beats, locations, and any important visual cues like camera angles or mood. The clearer your scene breakdown, the easier it is for the workflow to propose meaningful frames.

items.question: Can I choose different storyboard styles or fidelity levels?
items.answer: Yes. Depending on how the workflow is configured, you can lean toward rough sketch-style frames for fast planning or cleaner, more detailed panels when you need higher-fidelity boards for clients or internal reviews.

items.question: How do teams usually use AI-generated storyboards?
items.answer: Teams often use them to align directors, DPs, producers, and clients on camera plans, coverage, and overall flow before committing to full production. They are also helpful for pitching concepts and structuring YouTube or educational content.

items.question: Can I reorder or refine panels after the first generation?
items.answer: You can treat the first pass as a draft: reorder panels, adjust prompts or sections of your script, and then regenerate specific parts as needed to refine framing and pacing.

items.question: How do credits and costs work for One-Click Storyboard generations?
items.answer: Each storyboard generation consumes credits based on the length and settings of the workflow. A good strategy is to start with shorter sequences or rougher styles, then invest more credits into higher-fidelity boards once the structure feels right.
